title: Level 9.99

{{Class Custom Image|bg-image=BridgeSky.png|class=3e - Environmental|hexagon=🌪️|safety=Safe|security=Stable|entity=High numbers of tornadoes and few entities.}} '''Level 9.99''' is a sub-level in between [[Level 9]] and [[Level 10]] of the Backrooms.
Description
Level 9.99, also known as “Stormy Suburbs” or “Tornado Lands,” appears to be a stormy suburban complex. Its characteristics are the same as those of its parent level, Level 9. Level 9.99 is constantly shrouded in rain, consisting of a 48-hour cycle. Stores and other buildings can be found in certain locations throughout the level. These stores are usually empty or filled with remnants of dangerous objects or food. Some stores may have an infestation of pink larvae and other insects/entities due to the moist environment. The level presumably stretches out infinitely. {{blurimage|file=Suburb9.99.jpg|caption=Raging suburbs...|hovertext=I... I can't run from that.}} Every house within the level is completely furnished, with furniture that is in a perpetual state of cleanliness and always in prime condition. Additionally, every house contains an abnormally strong scent of detergent. The layout and design of these houses constantly change. These houses always seem to have 2 bedrooms, 2 bathrooms, 1 living room, 1 office, and 1 kitchen. The conditions within the basements are inhospitable for life; all drones will cut off if they go near the basement, and sometimes the basements are claustrophobic. There is close to no food in the house, making it bad to search. The weather on this level is completely unstable, with temperatures changing erratically from -10 to 17 degrees Celsius, and the weather may change to be hotter or colder depending on where you are. The rain is heavy, rarely letting up, almost never. The precipitation on this level is unpredictable, with no signs of warning before a storm forms. The level's terrain is at risk of collapsing due to the constant heavy rain that has been falling since its formation. Some parts of this level have already collapsed and are considered abandoned. Tornadoes are common within the level, usually occurring every 2 to 3 months. The level has a central region, known to many as “The Center,” which functions as the heart of level 9.99 due to its safe and stable infrastructure. There is less risk of collapse in this part of the level, but it is not entirely safe, as some tornadoes have hit the Center a few times, causing little damage even though it is an NS5 Category. Winds blow eastward, and many tornadoes usually form in the south, north, and east, with few in the west. These tornadoes that form in the west are usually a danger to the Center, as they mostly head eastward and end up hitting the Center. Several wealthy stores with reliable infrastructure can be found here, and many wanderers take shelter here, causing this area to suffer from overpopulation. The food in the Center is reliable and free of mold, fungi, and larvae, and is safe for consumption. The same is true for the water, which is uncontaminated and filtered. Unfortunately, many people die here due to the lack of space, air, and human causes. In this central region, there is another central region, in this case, the “Central Cone,” known by many as such because it is a cone, and many believe that it was because of this cone that the level was formed. {{blurimage|file=Suburbwithtornado.jpg|caption=A good time to sleep, don't you think?|hovertext=Ugh... what a headache!}} Research conducted by numerous organizations has revealed a major breakthrough-the level orbits a massive blackhole. The black hole that level 9.99 orbits around has been named "Abyssus". The perpetual rain has been caused by a phenomena unique to level 9.99. "Abyssus" seems to emit high levels of energy in the form of bolts of condensed energy, which causes massive storms to form. It is currently unknown why the energy produced by the black hole is converted into such a form of matter. Due to the effects of the black hole, the level experiences many chronological anomalies. Other research indicates that the atmosphere at this level is unstable, and the black hole resembles a quasar, as it shines brightly but emits almost no radiation. Furthermore, when a type of rocket camera (a camera designed to photograph above the atmosphere at this level) was launched, it found several stars, named “Gamma-Gycnus,” “Zelphegur-Gycnus,” “Nunk-Gycnus,” “Saphragor-Gycnus,” “Wügiu-Gycnus,” and “Dalmata-Gycnus.” They form the constellation Gycnus, until then the only known constellation that can be seen from the level, even though it is impossible to observe it directly from the level. Still, many people mention it and wonder what it would be like, among other things. Abyssus is also part of this constellation, even though it is far away. The stars that make up this constellation orbit this black hole along with the level. Tornadoes are common within the level; however, their formation is linked to the black hole's gravitational pull. Though the level's estimated distance from the blackhole is immense, the gravitational pull of the black hole is so strong that the air currents near the top of the level's atmosphere can be manipulated by the blackhole at any time to form strong cyclones or tornadoes. Below is the “Noire Scale” that classifies how dangerous a tornado formed in level 9.99 is. The “Noire Scale” was created by Boston Noire, who had his name on the Noire Scale as a tribute to his creation. The full history of the Noire Scale itself is unknown, as Boston Noire was introverted and did not like to talk and share information with other people. He died in 2002 of pneumonia. Even so, some information has been revealed, namely why he wanted to do this, as he ended up discovering the level and describing it as a “realistic home” because it looked very similar to where he lived in [[The Frontrooms]]. He had a nature similar to [REDACTED] and was passionate about tornadoes. He had Ted Fujita, a human from the frontrooms who became well known for the Fujita Scale, so he decided to create his own, with “NS” as the acronym and classification, which works based on destruction, wind, and how long it lasted. It has been improved over time and already has several photographs of tornadoes that were marked at level 9.99. Several wanderers tried to replicate the Noire Scale, but they did not gain popularity, as they work based on the point of view of the wanderer who created them. Several wanderers also tried to create a story to satisfy their children's curiosity. Many believe their parents' stories, but do not know the true story itself. {{Underline|FFFFFF}} {| style="background:#4DFFFF; color: #191919" |- |'''NS0''' |'''Minor damage.''' [[File:Captura de tela 2025-10-31 013657.png|thumb|180px]] NS0 tornadoes are weak and often referred to as “ropes” or “snakes”; these tornadoes are thin and do not cause much damage to the city. Often associated with whirlwinds, there is a big difference between an NS0 tornado and a whirlwind. They cause winds between 6 and 18 km/h and can end up tearing off roofs, knocking down trees, and causing little damage. |} {| style="background:#FFFFD9; color: #191919" |- |'''NS1''' |'''Moderate damage''' [[File:Captura de tela 2025-10-31 014553.png|thumb|180px]] NS1 tornadoes cause moderate damage, generally causing little destruction, and are much larger than NS0 tornadoes. NS1 tornadoes tear half a house apart, throw furniture or objects into other houses, and it is no longer considered “safe” to stay near tornadoes after this level of tornado. These tornadoes usually reach speeds of 19 to 34 km/h. Although they are slightly more destructive than NS0 tornadoes, they are considered dangerous due to the debris thrown by these NS1 tornadoes, which fly at 67 km/h. |} {| style="background:#FFD98C; color: #191919" |- |'''NS2''' |'''Considerable damage''' [[File:Captura de tela 2025-10-31 014720.png|thumb|180px]] NS2 tornadoes cause considerable destruction. Capable of destroying an entire neighborhood in at least 5 minutes, their “walking” speed is 58 km/h. Their damage is moderate; as mentioned above, they can destroy neighborhoods in just 5 minutes, destroying a house, leaving only rubble that makes the house unrecognizable. These tornadoes reach speeds of 35 to 68 km/h. |} {| style="background:#FF9E59; color: #191919" |- |'''NS3''' |'''Severe damage''' [[File:Captura de tela 2025-10-31 013958.png|thumb|180px]] NS3 tornadoes are those that cause severe damage. They can destroy up to four neighborhoods during their lifetime. They uproot trees, knock down power poles, etc. These tornadoes are generally recognized as “silent tornadoes” because they make almost no sound, making them very dangerous if they form at night. They cause winds of 69 to 142 km/h, which is quite dangerous. |} {| style="background:#FF738A; color: #191919" |- |'''NS4''' |'''Devastating damage''' [[File:Captura de tela 2025-10-20 021149.png|thumb|180px]] NS4 tornadoes are terrible and very dangerous, as they can end up destroying part of level 9.99. Causing devastating damage, they can even throw debris from houses to different locations, causing further imminent destruction. They usually arrive at speeds of 143 to 233 km/h and can even pull raindrops into the center of the tornado.This also happens with NS5 tornadoes; in fact, NS5 tornadoes can even pull lightning into their center. |} {| style="background:#A188FC; color: #191919" |- |'''NS5''' |'''Incredible damage''' [[File:Captura de tela 2025-10-20 022041.png|thumb|180px]] NS5 tornadoes are the worst tornadoes that can occur at level 9.99, as they end up destroying a large part of the level. They are usually very rare and very large, reaching up to 6 km in diameter and over 230 km/h, tearing up much of the ground and destroying a large part of the suburban complexes. The destruction is so severe that it can tear up the foundations of houses. Debris flies at over 90 mph, causing even more damage and potentially hitting unsuspecting wanderers. |} {{Underline|FFFFFF}} Sirens have been installed throughout the level to warn wanderers of an incoming tornado. {{Audio|file=Siren Tornado.mp3|volume=1|secrettext=Huh?|=no|round=yes}} Tornadoes are extremely dangerous and unpredictable, so caution must be taken, although never enter basements in houses.
Addenda
Addenda are a source of records of tornadoes that have attracted attention and serve only to increase knowledge about what has occurred in recent times.
> Log #1 - Joystick.
Those suburbs...
'''NS5 Tornado, named Joystick in 1952 - The first NS5 Tornado registered'''
16:55 - Strange spiral in the sky begins to form

16:58 - Storm is getting stronger.
17:01 - Sirens begin to sound.
17:06 - A column of air begins to form in the sky.
17:11 - Tornado touches down.
17:14 - Tornado begins to grow larger
17:15 - Meteorologists indicate that the tornado is NS4.
17:18 - Meteorologists indicate that the tornado is NS5.
17:22 - Tornado reaches 1.6 km in diameter.
17:27 - Storm Chasers Twixtaxing begins to locate and record tornado NS5, named Joystick.
17:33 - Tornado begins to wreak havoc and reaches 3.9 km in diameter.
17:35 - Joystick has killed 4 wanderers so far.
17:39 - Joystick reaches its peak diameter, 4.4 km in diameter.
17:40 - Twixtaxing sends a photograph of Joystick to the meteorologists at IStocklogister.
{{blurimage|file=Joystick Tornado.png|caption=Joystick|hovertext=The Joystick...|align=center}}
17:44 - Joystick begins to lose strength.
17:45 - Joystick has reduced diameter to 3.6 km.
17:47 - Diameter reduced to 2.2 km.
17:49 - Diameter reduced to 643 meters.
17:50 - Diameter reduced to 341 meters.
17:52 - Joystick begins to fall apart.
17:53 - End of Tornado.
17:59 - Meteorologists indicate that the tornado was indeed NS5, caused the deaths of 22 people, occurred at night, destroyed 13 neighborhoods, tore up foundations, and sent debris flying into the air.
''05/25/1952''
----

> Log #2 - Ostu.
Those suburbs...
'''NS3 Tornado, named Ostu in 1954 - NS3 Tornado registered'''
13:08 - A large storm cloud forms.

13:11 - Storm is getting stronger.
13:12 - Sirens begin to sound.
13:13 - A column of air begins to form in the sky.
13:19 - Tornado touches down.
13:20 - Tornado begins on NS2 class.
13:22 - Strong winds begin to blow.
13:25 - Windows explode because of strong winds.
13:30 - Tornado begins to devastate two entire neighborhoods.
13:31 - Storm Chasers Twixtaxing begins to record the NS3 tornado, named Ostu
13:34 - The tornado ended up hitting another neighborhood.
13:40 - Tornado begins to weaken.
13:42 - Twixtaxing sent a photo to the meteorologists at IStocklogister when it was weak, before it dissipated.
{{blurimage|file=Ostu Tornado.png|caption=Ostu Tornado|hovertext=Ostu...|align=center}}
13:43 - Tornado ends up dissipating.
13:44 - Meteorologists indicate that the tornado was indeed NS3, caused the deaths of 5 people, occurred during the day, destroyed 3 neighborhoods, and scattered debris across the three neighborhoods.
''12/12/1954''
----

> Log #3 - BridgeSky.
Those suburbs...
'''NS5 Tornado, named BridgeSky in 2014 - Last and Largest NS5 Tornado registered'''
04:33 - A large storm cloud forms.

04:41 - Storm is getting stronger.
04:42 - Sirens begin to sound.
04:47 - A column of air begins to form in the sky.
04:50 - Tornado touches down.
04:52 - The Tornado barely touched the ground and destroyed two houses.
04:53 - Strong winds begin to blow.
04:57 - Tornado begins to gain strength and size.
05:01 - Storm chasers ViewTwisters begin observing the tornado.
05:02 - Twixtaxing arrives to start recording and observing the tornado along with two other groups of Storm Chasers, GazGam Tornado-Record and ZeusFuriosoT.
05:03 - Twixtaxing gives the order to retreat.
05:06 - Tornado reaches 5 km in diameter.
05:07 - Tornado has already destroyed 7 neighborhoods and killed 74 wanderers.
05:10 - Tornado reaches 7 km in diameter.
05:11 - ZeusFuriosoT's car is caught and kills 4 members.
05:13 - Twixtaxing named it BridgeSky (Bridge because it looked like a bridge in the sky, due to it being a twin tornado, i.e., two tornadoes on either side when viewed from a distance.) (Sky because, as mentioned earlier, it looked like a bridge in the sky.)
05:18 - Tornado reaches 9 km in diameter.
05:21 - IStockLogister indicates that the tornado's diameter is expected to decrease and dissipate in less than 1 hour.
05:22 - GazGam Tornado-Record takes a photo of BridgeSky.
05:24 - Photo arrives at IStockLogister.
{{Blurimage|file=BridgeSky.png|caption=The tornado was so large that it took six photographs to determine the diameter of BridgeSky.|hovertext=BridgeSky... 9.6km...|align=center}}
05:27 - Tornado begins to decrease in size.
05:28 - The tornado quickly changes direction, moving westward, then southward. It is heading toward the center of the level.
05:30 - Unsuspecting wanderers die without even knowing what happened.
05:31 - Two wanderers were killed by trampling.
05:32 - Tornado reaches 6 km in diameter.
05:34 - Even though it is 6 km in diameter, it begins to loosen and dissipate from the ground.
05:35 - The second twin tornado meets and merges with the other tornado, slightly increasing BridgeSky's diameter from 6 km to 8.3 km.
05:36 - BridgeSky begins to dissipate abnormally, even with its 8 km diameter.
05:39 - BridgeSky finally dissipates, leaving behind a trail of catastrophic destruction where the twin tornadoes passed.
05:45 - Meteorologists indicate that the tornado, named BridgeSky by Twixtaxing, photographed by GazGam Tornado-Record, killed 137 wanderers directly and 9 indirectly, totaling 146 dead wanderers. It was an NS5 tornado, which had not occurred since 2001, causing mass destruction, which in [[The Frontrooms]] would cause $3.6 billion in damage.
''04/30/2014''
----

> Log #4 - Naja Mortis.
Those suburbs...
'''NS5 Tornado, named Naja Mortis in 2001 - The deadliest tornado ever recorded.'''
21:47 - A large storm cloud forms.

21:52 - Storm is getting stronger.
22:00 - IStockLogister indicates that a cyclone has begun to form near the center.
22:04 - A column of air begins to form in the sky.
22:07 - Tornado touches down.
22:11 - The tornado formed in the east of the center.
22:12 - Strong winds begin to blow.
22:15 - Tornado reaches [[Level 969|969]] meters in diameter and kills 6 people.
22:18 - All groups of storm chasers arrive to record and document the tornado.
22:22 - Tornado reaches 1.4 km in diameter and has already killed 83 wanderers.
22:29 - The tornado reaches its peak size of 1.7 km in diameter.
22:33 - The tornado has begun to decrease in size and is moving toward the center.
22:35 - The tornado comes very close to the center and is 1.2 km in diameter.
22:37 - Tornado hits the center, killing more than 60 wanderers.
22:41 - Research indicates that at this time, it ripped off several roofs and caused some damage in parts of the city center.
22:44 - Twixtaxing, when he saw the damage and how many people had died in this tornado, named it “Naja Mortis.”
22:45 - ViewTwisters took a photo when the tornado was only 1,040 meters in diameter (1.04 km).
22:46 - Photo arrives in the IStockLogister database.
{{Blurimage|file=NS5 Naja Mortis.jpg|caption=Debris from a building in the center. Note that in the image, the sky is slightly cloudy; this sometimes occurs after a cyclone dissipates very quickly.|hovertext=Naja Mortis...|align=center}}
22:48 - The tornado reached 610 meters in diameter and began to enter a rope stage.
22:57 - The tornado begins to thin out so much that it becomes almost impossible to see with the naked eye from a distance of 32 meters.
23:00 - Tornado begins to dissipate.
23:02 - The tornado finally dissipated.
23:20 - Data recorded by IStockLogister and storm chasers indicate that it would cause around $800 million in damage if it occurred in front rooms. It killed a total of 201 direct victims and another 11 to 12 indirect victims, making it the deadliest in the history of level 9.99 recorded. Winds reached >490 km/h. Tornado classified as NS5. It occurred on May 21, 2001.
''05/21/2001''
----


Colonies & Outposts
Groups at this level are often referred to as storm chasers, meteorologists, wanderers, group bases, etc. They are designed to help wanderers and study phenomena at this level. =
Twixtaxing
= {{Underline|FFFFFF}} Twixtaxing is the leading group of storm chasers, having been formed in 1947; it is the first group of storm chasers at level 9.99. Its history comes from the brilliant mind of Theodore Muutsky, who was a tornado hunter in the frontrooms, who ended up falling into the backrooms. He hunted tornadoes at level 10.4, but unfortunately had the misfortune of noclipping into a gray wheat field, so he reached level 9.99. Realizing that there were more people at that level, he was happy. So in 1947, he founded Twixtaxing with other storm chasers. His journey takes place here. Boston Noire was also part of this group. Realizing that he could no longer stay, he was the first person to leave Twixtaxing. The group became better known for reporting and photographing the Joystick and Ostu tornado. Today, with new technologies within this level, they are able to photograph and study level 9.99 tornadoes better. Today, if we talk about how the group has grown, it is incomparable to others, as they are generous and accept anyone who has good experience in the niche of storm chasing. With 55 members, the group has now become well-known at level 9.99, as IStockLogister and several wanderers now trust Twixtaxing. =
ViewTwisters
= {{Underline|FFFFFF}} ViewTwisters are a group of storm chasers who aim to photograph tornadoes up close, hence the name ViewTwisters. Their goals are clear, as they are more focused on photographing tornadoes. Unlike other groups of tornado hunters, their photographs are the best, as they know how to position the camera very well, including making videos. This group has 38 members. To be part of it, you must be the best at photographing tornadoes, maintaining stability in the best possible position. Their priorities are cameras and car fortifications, as they focus more on photographing tornadoes, so they study based on what they see in the photographs. =
GazGam Tornado-Record (GGTR)
= {{Underline|FFFFFF}} One of the newest storm chasing groups, GazGam Tornado-Record (GGTR), has 19 active members and 6 inactive members, for a total of 25 members. It is also the only group that has not had any members killed to date. The group is characterized by its focus on safety. Since several wanderers have been rescued from debris by GazGam Tornado-Record, they rarely take photos, and when they do, it is more to show the danger than to provide information. This prioritization of safety at GazGam Tornado-Record has meant that no members have died to date. They also warn people about the danger if they do not want to leave their homes and seek a safe place. =
ZeusFuriosoT
= {{Underline|FFFFFF}} ZeusFuriosoT, Portuguese for Furious Zeus and T for Tornado, is a group of 12 storm chasers and the one with the fewest members. ZeusFuriosoT does not have good cars for hunting tornadoes. After BridgeSky, ZeusFuriosoT lost 4 of its 16 members, leaving it with 12 members. As a result, they began to take more precautions when hunting tornadoes. These drastic measures were necessary for them to avoid losing more members. However, ZeusFuriosoT began a new phase of testing, creating new cars and starting to modernize things. =
IStockLogister
= {{Underline|FFFFFF}} IStockLogister is a group of 43 meteorologists specializing in tornadoes, landslides, and climate change. Their work is usually displayed on various posters within the level, typically in the center. IStockLogister also serves as a photo gallery, as several photographs of tornadoes are stored in the IStockLogister database; these photographs are carefully analyzed, noting what it was, whether it was a mere storm, a subcyclone, cyclone, or a tornado. Its base is located underground, in a tunnel in the “Central Cone” of the level, ensuring that it is very safe. =
Wanderers of Tornadoes
= {{Underline|FFFFFF}} Wanderers who enter Level 9.99 are recognized as tornado wanderers. This is not a group per se, but it functions as a civilization. Even though the level is class 3 - Environmental, many wanderers consider it safe, as there are almost no entities, and due to the numerous active sirens on the level, it ends up being safe. This is also because the level has several tornadoes, so everyone must be very attentive at the very least. In addition to wanderers who are natives of regions where tornadoes occur frequently, there are several wanderers who pass through the level to relax. As mentioned earlier, wanderers who are natives of regions where tornadoes occur frequently or are more accustomed to them usually call this place “Realistic Home.” =
[[M.E.G.]] Base StormWatcher
= {{Underline|FFFFFF}} {{Login|organization=M.E.G.|username={{USERNAME}}|password=Uz30@G4WIkjgoku|body=... Access denied :( '''Reason:''' Information redacted.}}
Entities
Entities are hostile and passive presences. Few entities are present at this level. =
[[Entity 49|Pink Larvae]]
= Pink larvae appear in foods that are more moist than normal. If you are unaware, foods with pink larvae should be eaten with caution and, if possible, avoided at all costs. It is worth noting that all foods infested with pink larvae are found in regions other than the Center. As mentioned earlier, the food in the Center is clean, monitored, and the water is safe to drink. If you find or suspect food that may have pink larvae, it is recommended that you throw it away so that your body does not become infested with pink larvae. {{Underline|FFFFFF}} =
Residents
= Residents are a unique entity at this level. Their appearance resembles that of a person close to the wanderer. Their behavior is very predictable and easy to identify; they always stand at a window observing level 9.99. Houses containing residents are always locked, sometimes with characters in another language. This language that is found probably came from long ago; its words are different, but it seems that when spoken, it sounds like Russian. These characters found in these houses look like a combination of Russian, Japanese, Roman, Vietnamese, and indigenous peoples' characters (found in [[The Frontrooms]]). {{Underline|FFFFFF}} =
[[Entity 74|Tornadoes]]
= Tornadoes are one of the most common features on the level; however, almost none of them are entities, but rather phenomena on the level. To know if a tornado is an entity, make sure that it is mostly purple and that you can see a type of kinetic energy in the center of the tornado.
Environments
Environmental hazards or Environments are NOT phenomena or anomalies in themselves, as they are part of the ecosystem of the level and formation, and are also one of the environmental characteristics of the level. ===
Rain
=== One notable feature of level 9.99 is its constant rain, which never stops. This rain is known to be one of the most common environmental hazards at this level, as the ground can collapse due to the constant rain, becoming weak and very heavy. The rain also causes viral risks to wanderers or other things, as they are identical to those in [[The Frontrooms]]. So they can carry diseases that have been frozen in the dense clouds of the level. However, it is not known how the clouds created these diseases. It can cause pneumonia and colds. Several wanderers who spent more than 12 hours in the rain experienced chills, colds, fever, and even pneumonia in more severe cases. Therefore, it is not recommended to wander around the level for more than 12 hours without proper equipment or clothing. This rain, along with the winds, is blowing westward. Western areas are more prone to landslides due to the constant accumulation of rain, but even so, the chances are still low because, as mentioned earlier, the level extends infinitely. {{Underline|FFFFFF}} ===
Cyclone
=== Cyclones are one of the most common phenomena seen at this level and are one of the main causes of storms and tornadoes that occur at this level. Their characteristics are due to the fact that they rotate clockwise rather than counterclockwise. If you encounter one, it does not guarantee that a tornado will occur, as sometimes they only cause more lightning strikes. One thing to note is that these electrical charges generated by cyclones do not cause thunderThunder is the noise made by an electrical discharge., probably due to the heavy rain that occurs beneath the cyclone, which ends up muffling the thunder. LightningLightning is the electrical discharge itself. is often seen inside the cyclone, even if there are not many electrical discharges, due to the fact that time passes more slowly at that level, and the speed of light is also reduced. Cyclones are usually seen as lower, a kind of circle or upside-down cap in the sky, spinning and spinning. Cyclones occur when there is great instability in the affected cloud, due to a secondary gust that, when it occurs, ends up destabilizing the cloud, lowering its level, that is, a cyclone. {{Underline|FFFFFF}} ===
Mesocyclone
=== Mesocyclones are practically a “subcyclone” since that is essentially what they are. The only thing that differentiates them is their low altitude, and if mesocyclones appear, a tornado can be expected to follow. Their characteristics are their low altitude and, being smaller and rotating counterclockwise, they are indicative of a tornado appearing. Mesocyclones usually appear when two or more molecules of positive ion charges merge, becoming larger and more unstable. The same is true for negative ion charges; when these two unstable charges merge, they generate a third superactive ion charge, which ends up condensing in the main cyclone, destabilizing it and creating a second, smaller cyclone that rotates counterclockwise. Since cyclones are made entirely of positive ion charges, rain carries negative ion charges, which merge with everything and form electrical discharges. But in a mesocyclone, due to the general instability of these molecules, which begin to approach and merge, they create a type of drain or hole in the center of the mesocyclone, which, inside it, creates ideal conditions for the formation of tornadoes. {{Underline|FFFFFF}} ===
Tornado
=== Tornadoes, one of the most common phenomena in the level, are responsible for all of the level's characteristics. One of the most impressive phenomena seen throughout the level is caused by a current of hot, humid air created by plants rising, and the cold, dry air current wants to take its place, causing general instability and severe depression in that area. Witnesses say they “smell a strange odor and feel low blood pressure and shortness of breath” when they are inside the phenomenon known as “Air Stroke” or “Air Break.” It is a rare phenomenon that generates small whirlwinds because they cannot find a place to rise in the dense clouds, and also because there are no positively and negatively charged ion molecules present in the clouds, so it is rare to observe an electrical discharge. When “Air Stroke” or “Air Break” occurs below a mesocyclone, charged with positive and negative ions, warm air enters the mesocyclone and cyclone and exits through the outside air. When this occurs, the warm air above the clouds is pulled down by the positive ion charges, returning with a low-pressure area, and when Abyssus ends up creating condensation gusts, nearby or on site, the negative ion charges descend, causing a type of “drain” inside the cyclone and mesocyclone, since they all want to rise, but the constant stream of hot air descending through the “drain” does not allow these molecules and currents to rise back up, and the wind ends up changing direction drastically, the area begins to spin, and this is so strong that it ends up rotating the surrounding clouds, called “wind shear.” This phenomenon contributes to the formation of the tornado, rotating clouds and condensed gases to the ground, creating a funnel, or vortex, which, when it touches the ground, is officially called a tornado. Another thing to note is a multiple vortex tornado, which, in its formation, ends up causing a change in negative charges, which begin to accumulate at various points, creating sub-vortexes that rotate around the tornado due to “wind shear.” Satellite tornadoes are tornadoes SEPARATE from the main tornado. They are smaller versions and often do not exceed NS3. However, they can be dangerous if ignored, as they are much more unstable than the main tornadoes themselves. They are the opposite of multiple vortices, where positive ion charges and heat molecules multiply rapidly in a region, which can cause a second tornado. An important point to note is the so-called “twin tornadoes,” which are two relatively parallel and close cyclones that have the same formation as a tornado. Often, twin tornadoes have a reduced duration, and it is not known why. Another type of tornado formation is the gravitational pull of black holes, which end up drawing in condensed gases and parts of clouds; this phenomenon occurs in the most distant parts of the black hole, meaning it always forms at night. It is not known why this only occurs in these parts, and tornadoes formed in this way are cone-shaped or inverted funnel-shaped, pulling clouds downwards and rotating clockwise. Tornadoes formed in this way are more violent and cause more destruction, and are called “Abyssal Tornadoes.”Abyssal comes from Abyssus, the supermassive black hole that causes Abyssal Tornadoes. Again, tornadoes are unpredictable. Many wanderers who underestimated or deluded themselves/played the hero, died because they were unaware of the danger. {{Underline|FFFFFF}} ===
Temperature
=== Temperatures at level 9.99 vary dramatically and are cold, since, as mentioned earlier, there are many cold air currents, most of them humid and a few dry. This occurs because warm air currents are above the clouds, and also because cold air molecules are more present at this level due to the fact that the droplets do not reflect much, and the greenhouse effect only occurs above the clouds. The opposite effect occurs: cold air molecules are closer together and more condensed, thus making the air colder, and also because they are very stable. Now, warm air molecules are more separated and unstable, and many cold air molecules prevent them from coming together or staying close, not causing as much of an effect as cold air molecules do at that level. {{Underline|FFFFFF}} ===
Plants
=== Any type of plant, such as trees or other types, such as cabbage, lettuce, etc., is responsible for creating warm air currents, unlike some cases where warm air molecules remain stable and merge, as in 1615. They function mainly for the water cycle, which, at this level, only makes rain possible because of the trees. Cold air currents enter the leaves of plants, which end up “filtering” them in a type of “nephron,” which ends up catching cold air molecules and sending them back into the air, purer, and bringing the hot air molecules together and stable, creating hot air currents. Plants are also part of the water cycle, which begins with the black hole releasing bursts of condensed air, turning into clouds; then, the cold air currents are filtered and warm air currents end up passing through and melting frozen water, which ends up in a liquid state, and some hot air molecules get trapped in the water, so the water freezes again, and the hot air molecules begin to become unstable, producing steam. This steam rises, and in the clouds, the air is hot inside them, producing raindrops, which fall and form precipitation at ground level. The cycle repeats itself, forming the rain we know at ground level. In addition, all plants are edible.
Anomalies & phenomena.
Anomalies and phenomena are precisely the opposite of the Environmental. ===
Rain Man
=== {{Phenomenon Class|frequency=4|effectiveness=2|danger=3}} There exists a peculiar anomaly that may occur within this level, known as the Rain Man phenomenon. According to witness accounts, the Rain Men manifest as figures resembling average-sized human males composed entirely of rain with an ever-present puddle beneath their feet. This phenomenon typically appears at the edge of one’s peripheral vision. If ignored, it will vanish after approximately ten seconds; however, when an observer turns to face it, the entity will charge forward at an inhuman speed. When it reaches a distance of roughly fifty feet, it appears to dive into the ground. Just before doing so, the phenomenon transforms into an expanding tidal wave, which subsequently drenches and knocks over the wanderer. {{Underline|FFFFFF}} ===
Cars
=== {{Phenomenon Class|frequency=5|effectiveness=5|danger=0}} An anomaly known as level 9.99 is called “Cars.” They are one of the most common anomalies seen at this level. Cars at this level can be seen in photos, videos, and any type of observation. Their characteristics are similar to those of a moving car; they have high beams, usually black in color. They always follow a straight line on the level and are often driverless. Notably, no equipment or objects are seen inside these cars, and they appear to be made of plastic, a material that is not very resistant to high speeds. Cars hit by tornadoes or very strong winds are seen “disfigured” and unrecognizable, often separated into several parts, and these parts are seen hundreds of meters away. None of them causes any kind of physical damage or accident, because, as mentioned earlier, they always follow a straight line, and if one does occur, no injuries occur, as they are only indicated by psychiatrists as a figment of the imagination. {{Underline|FFFFFF}} ===
Sudden Greening
=== {{Phenomenon Class|frequency=4|effectiveness=5|danger=1}} One of the phenomena that can be seen at this level is sudden greening. Sudden greening occurs when there is high tension between carbon dioxide and nitrogen molecules in the air, which end up merging with some atoms in the atmosphere, causing instability in the quarks of the atoms, which end up condensing gases in the atmosphere. And when the air inside these condensation gases is cold, the water droplets scatter the light generated by the black hole. When these condensation gases are very dense, they end up absorbing red and blue light, allowing only green light to pass through. This occurs most often when there is a mesocyclone or a tornado.
Entrances & Exits
There are a few entrances and exits, but they are easily accessible. =
Entrances
= {{Underline|FFFFFF}} * Finding an umbrella and using it in a shower in [[Level 9|a darkened suburban city]] will lead here. * Entering a storm shelter within [[Level 241|the turbine fields]] has a small chance to lead one to a storm shelter within this level. * Noclipping into gray wheat at [[Level 10.4|crop season]] will lead here. * Sleeping in a house for too long in [[Level 55|the mindless hills]] will cause one to wake up in a house within the suburbs. * Finding a rundown house in [[Level 992|a 2000s-era suburb]] will lead one here. * Rarely, when it rains at any level, one will enter here. * If a random tidal wave appears on any beach level, it has a 3% chance to send one here. =
Exits
= {{Underline|FFFFFF}} * Finding a musty, yellow door will lead to [[Level 0|an infinite single-yellow maze]]. * Touching a wind turbine will lead to [[Level 241|turbine fields]]. * Finding a wheat field near a barn house will lead to [[Level 10|an infinite wheat field]]. * Finding an urban structure will lead to [[Level 11|an urban city]] or [[Level 84|a homogenous skyline]]. * Finding a purple mushroom will lead to [[Level 626|the psychosis]]. * Entering a drainage pipe can lead you to [[Level 883|dark and filthy sewers.]] * Finding a cave will lead to another level resembling [[Level 8|a cave]], but if the cave found here is slightly colder than usual, and some parts are frozen, it will lead to [[Level 202.1|frozen caves]]. * Finding a relatively contemporary house will lead you to [[Level 53|a contemporary house]].
What was that?

???: Hey! You there! Want to eat with us?
You get confused... trying to understand what they mean...
???: Come on! Or do you want to get hit by a tornado?
{{USERNAME}}: Tornado??
You become even more confused, because you didn't know... that there were tornadoes at that level...
Until you feel stronger winds... and stronger... and stronger...
???: Didn't you know there were tornadoes at this level?
{{USERNAME}}: How am I supposed to trust you guys?
You decide not to trust them, so you go to an empty house...
And you see... that the tornado begins to form.
That tornado...
That tornado...
That tornado...
That tornado began to devastate everything.
And he comes closer...
closer...
closer...
closer...
closer...
and closer...
That tornado was... surreal!
But you're not surprised...
All you feel now is fear...
more fear...
more fear...
more fear...
more fear...
more fear...
more fear...
and... more fear...
Until that cloud of debris hits you squarely...
You're now just begging for it to pass quickly and not take your life with it...
Wait, you hear a popping sound inside your ears...The ears are organs that are sensitive to air pressure. The middle ear is an air-filled cavity that is connected to the back of the throat by a tube called the Eustachian tube. The Eustachian tube helps to equalize the air pressure in the middle ear with the air pressure in the environment. When air pressure changes, the Eustachian tube may not be able to equalize the air pressure in the middle ear with the air pressure in the environment. This can cause a pressure difference between the middle ear and the environment, which can lead to a popping or crackling sound in the ear.
What could have caused that? It echoes in his thoughts...
But you see...
You realize you're in the eye of the tornado...
You hold on tighter to your bathtub and your mattress... since they are the only things protecting you so far...
That sound of a train moving loudly is now echoing in your ears and thoughts...
But after a while... You feel that the winds have weakened... your fear has now weakened... that sound of a moving train is farther away...
And that pop in your ear occurs... You emerge from the rubble and your bathtub...
And you see that little cabin didn't survive... where they were inviting you...
You look around... all that trail of destruction... You faint...
When you wake up... you're in some kind of hospital... without knowing... that you were inside a tornado and survived...
You thank God for having survived... And now... everything will remain in your mind... until you die.
What was that?
